posts.title: Building a Blog with Go
posts.description: How I built this blog engine
posts.content: Building a Blog with Go I built this blog engine from scratch using Go. Here's how it works: Architecture myblog/ ├── cmd/ # CLI entry point ├── internal/ # Core packages │ ├── config/ # Configuration │ ├── content/ # Markdown parsing │ ├── generator/ # HTML generation │ ├── search/ # Search index │ └── feed/ # RSS/Atom ├── themes/ # Theme templates └── content/ # Blog posts Key Packages Markdown Parsing Using goldmark for Markdown to HTML conversion with extensions for: GFM (GitHub Flavored Markdown) Syntax highlighting Tables and task lists Templating Go's built-in html/template package provides safe templating with: Layouts and partials Custom functions Automatic escaping Frontmatter YAML frontmatter in each post provides metadata: Title, date, tags Description Draft status Results The blog generates static HTML that's fast, secure, and easy to host anywhere!

posts.title: Static Site Generators
posts.content: Static Site Generators Static site generators have become popular again. Here's why they're excellent choice: Benefits Speed - Pre-rendered HTML loads instantly Security - No database or server-side code to hack Simplicity - Just files, easy to version control Scalability - CDN hosting, nearly unlimited traffic Popular Options Hugo - Very fast, written in Go Jekyll - Ruby-based, GitHub Pages friendly Eleventy - Node.js based, flexible This blog - Custom Go implementation! When to Use Static Blogs and portfolios Documentation sites Landing pages Any content that doesn't need real-time data Static sites aren't right for every use case, but when they work, they work beautifully.

posts.title: Markdown Features
posts.content: Markdown Features Here are some of the Markdown features supported by this blog: Lists Unordered list: Item one Item two Item three Ordered list: First item Second item Third item Code Blocks Inline code and code blocks: def hello(): print("Hello from Python!") function hello() { console.log("Hello from JavaScript!"); } Blockquotes This is a blockquote. It can be used for quotes or highlights. Multiple lines work too! Tables Feature Support Tables Yes Code Yes Links Yes Links and Images Visit Go website Task Lists Create blog engine Add Markdown support Write more posts Deploy to production

posts.title: Hello World
posts.content: Hello World Welcome to my new blog! This is my first post written in Markdown. Why Go? I chose Go for this blog engine because: Fast compilation - Go compiles very quickly Simple syntax - Easy to read and maintain Great standard library - Lots of built-in functionality Static binaries - No runtime dependencies package main import "fmt" func main() { fmt.Println("Hello, World!") } This is just the beginning. Stay tuned for more posts about Go, web development, and more!
